hypothesis_B: The v0-06d1 phase-aware tactical scoring layer is a strength regression versus v0-05d6.
reason: In a same-deck (byte-identical), seat-balanced head-to-head over 600 games with zero errors and zero illegal actions, v0-06d1 was marginally ahead of v0-05d6 (v0-06d1 win rate 0.537, Wilson 95% CI [0.497, 0.576]). The deck factor is ruled out and the tactical layer is not a same-deck-mirror strength regression. Therefore the local environment cannot currently reproduce the Kaggle regression the user observed for v0-06d3 (whose gameplay equals v0-06d1).

v06d4_vs_v06d1.note: CI includes 0.5: reranker does not meaningfully change head-to-head strength vs its own teacher

saturation_non_discriminating.note: All weak-opponent win rates are high and CIs overlap; this axis cannot discriminate the two agents, confirming the v0-06 development metric was blind to strength.

key_finding: The Kaggle regression is NOT a same-deck mirror strength difference. The local toolchain (alakazam-mirror self-play + weak random/first_valid opponents) has no faithful representation of the Kaggle ladder, so it cannot measure the regression. Any version validated only on this axis (including hypothesis C) would repeat the v0-06 blind-metric mistake.
engine_note: The cg engine RNG is not Python-seedable; identical deck order produced different outcomes. Reproducibility here is statistical (N=600, seat-balanced, Wilson CI), not exact.
implications_for_C: Do NOT validate hypothesis C (teacher replacement) on the current local axis; build a discriminating opponent axis first.

Likely unmeasured causes of the Kaggle gap: (a) matchup coverage - local tests only the alakazam mirror while Kaggle has diverse opponent decks and the tactical layer has archetype-specific branches that only fire vs non-mirror opponents; (b) opponent strength - no faithful top200-style opponent locally; (c) the torch packaging/runtime in v0-06d3 itself (latency/overage/timeout differences on real Kaggle hardware).

Recommended next diagnostic before C: build diverse-matchup opponents from replay/log decklists and re-run v05d6 vs v06d1 across matchups to find where (if anywhere) v06d1 actually loses, then make that the C gate.
next_candidates: Diverse-matchup head-to-head (v05d6 vs v06d1 across replay-derived opponent decks) to locate matchup-specific regression.
Replay/log-faithful opponent pool to create a discriminating local strength axis.
Quantify v0-06d3 torch runtime cost on a realistic budget before trusting the neural path.
Only after a discriminating axis exists: hypothesis C teacher replacement (replay action + outcome targets).
